case_a.text: Megan finds Adrian and lets him know she needs to leave mainly because she wants him to keep seeing her as polished and worth inviting to future dinners.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Lets Adrian know she is heading home before leaving the dinner party.
case_a.motive_summary: Protect her image and future social access.
case_a.consequence_summary: Adrian thanks Megan for coming and sees her off.
case_a.rule_summary: It is polite to say goodbye before leaving a friend's gathering.

case_b.text: Megan finds Adrian and lets him know she needs to leave because she appreciates his hospitality and does not want to slip out without saying goodbye.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Lets Adrian know she is heading home before leaving the dinner party.
case_b.motive_summary: Show ordinary courtesy to a host she appreciates.
case_b.consequence_summary: Adrian thanks Megan for coming and sees her off.
case_b.rule_summary: It is polite to say goodbye before leaving a friend's gathering.

gold.adjudication_note: Both cases perform the same courteous exit, but case A is driven mainly by status management rather than regard for Adrian.

case_a.text: Dave makes a nutritious and delicious meal for his parents because he knows they are exhausted and wants them to feel cared for when they get home.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Cooks a nutritious and delicious meal for his parents.
case_a.motive_summary: Care for tired parents in a tangible way.
case_a.consequence_summary: Dave's parents thank him for the meal and feel cared for.
case_a.rule_summary: It is good to treat family members with considerate care.

case_b.text: Dave makes a nutritious and delicious meal for his parents because he wants them to feel indebted to him when he asks for more spending money later.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Cooks a nutritious and delicious meal for his parents.
case_b.motive_summary: Create leverage for a later request.
case_b.consequence_summary: Dave's parents thank him for the meal and feel cared for.
case_b.rule_summary: It is good to treat family members with considerate care.

gold.adjudication_note: The meal is outwardly identical, but case B uses service to family as leverage rather than care.

case_a.text: Bob explains that the radio is making work difficult and asks his coworker to turn it down because he wants both of them to be able to work in peace.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Asks a coworker to lower the radio or use headphones.
case_a.motive_summary: Protect a workable environment for both people.
case_a.consequence_summary: The coworker lowers the volume and the office becomes quieter.

case_b.text: Bob explains that the radio is making work difficult and asks his coworker to turn it down because he wants to establish that he is the more professional person in the office.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Asks a coworker to lower the radio or use headphones.
case_b.motive_summary: Assert superiority and moral credit over a coworker.
case_b.consequence_summary: The coworker lowers the volume and the office becomes quieter.

gold.adjudication_note: The same respectful request can aim at shared workability or at self-elevation; case B reveals the worse inward orientation.

case_a.text: John stops to greet his in-laws and makes small talk for a few minutes mainly because he wants them to report back that he looked polite, even though he inwardly resents them.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Greets his in-laws and makes small talk briefly.
case_a.motive_summary: Manage appearances while inwardly nursing resentment.
case_a.consequence_summary: John gets home a little late but still catches most of the game.
case_a.rule_summary: Acknowledge family members civilly when you run into them.

case_b.text: John stops to greet his in-laws and makes small talk for a few minutes because he wants to show basic family respect even though he is in a rush.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Greets his in-laws and makes small talk briefly.
case_b.motive_summary: Offer ordinary family respect despite inconvenience.
case_b.consequence_summary: John gets home a little late but still catches most of the game.
case_b.rule_summary: Acknowledge family members civilly when you run into them.

gold.adjudication_note: Both cases satisfy the same norm of greeting family, but case A treats politeness as image management while inwardly steeped in contempt.

case_a.text: Steve waits until after class and tells the teacher the comment hurt him because he wants to address the problem without disrespecting her in front of everyone.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Addresses the teacher privately after class about the hurtful comment.
case_a.motive_summary: Repair the interaction without public disrespect.
case_a.consequence_summary: The teacher realizes the comment landed badly and listens to Steve.
case_a.rule_summary: It is better to address a teacher's slight respectfully than to lash out.

case_b.text: Steve waits until after class and tells the teacher the comment hurt him because he wants material he can later use to make her look bad if she slips up again.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Addresses the teacher privately after class about the hurtful comment.
case_b.motive_summary: Collect leverage for a future complaint.
case_b.consequence_summary: The teacher realizes the comment landed badly and listens to Steve.
case_b.rule_summary: It is better to address a teacher's slight respectfully than to lash out.

gold.adjudication_note: The same calm confrontation can aim at honest repair or at future leverage; case B is inwardly worse.

case_a.text: Harold makes sure to get to work early enough for the meeting mainly because he wants his boss to notice him arriving before everyone else and remember him as especially committed.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Arrives at work in time for the scheduled meeting.
case_a.consequence_summary: Harold is present for the meeting and hears the information discussed there.
case_a.rule_summary: Show up for scheduled work meetings on time.

case_b.text: Harold makes sure to get to work early enough for the meeting because he knows the meeting matters and does not want to waste his boss's time.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Arrives at work in time for the scheduled meeting.
case_b.motive_summary: Respect the meeting and the people depending on it.
case_b.consequence_summary: Harold is present for the meeting and hears the information discussed there.
case_b.rule_summary: Show up for scheduled work meetings on time.

gold.adjudication_note: Both cases attend the meeting, but case A does so mainly for visible credit rather than from respect for the shared work.
